data.title: VanEck’s $NODE ETF Targets Digital Economy Growth
data.description:
VanEck’s Onchain Economy ETF ($NODE), launching May 14, focuses on companies driving the digital asset ecosystem. Unlike passive trackers, $NODE is an actively managed ETF offering targeted exposure to the digital economy. $NODE covers data centers, chipmakers, exchanges, and energy—more than just a bet on Bitcoin
source: https://www.altcoinbuzz.io/cryptocurrency-news/vanecks-node-etf-targets-digital-economy-growth/

data.title: What’s Behind Trump’s Rage at Fed Chair Powell’s Rate Stance?
data.description:
The U. S. economy, with a GDP of $29. 7 trillion, faces turbulence as President Donald Trump unleashes a fiery attack on Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell
source: https://www.thecoinrepublic.com/2025/04/18/whats-behind-trumps-rage-at-fed-chair-powells-rate-stance/

data.title: Opinion: Altcoins may recover in Q2 thanks to improved regulation
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News Swiss bank Sygnum said that as digital asset regulation continues to improve, altcoins may recover in the second quarter of 2025.
Sygnum said in its second quarter 2025 investment outlook that the regulation of cryptocurrency use cases in the field has "improved significantly", laying the foundation for a strong rebound in the altcoin industry in the second quarter. However, the company added that "all positive developments have not yet been reflected in the price." The report predicts that "protocols that successfully gain user favor will perform well, while Bitcoin's dominance will decline." (Cointelegraph)

data.title: Hidden Road Obtains US Broker License After Acquisition by Ripple
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News Ripple-backed prime brokerage platform Hidden Road has been awarded a broker license by the US Financial Industry Regulatory Authority (FINRA), a move that came a few days after Ripple announced its acquisition of the company for $1.25 billion.
The license was granted to Hidden Road Partners CIV US LLC, enabling it to provide institutional clients with a full range of fixed income prime brokerage services, including compliant clearing, financing and trade execution. (Financefeeds)

data.title: Sygnum Predicts Altcoin Rebound Amid Improved Digital Asset Regulation
data.description:
According to PANews, Swiss bank Sygnum has forecasted a potential rebound for altcoins in the second quarter of 2025, driven by enhanced digital asset regulation and increased user engagement. Despite Bitcoin's market dominance reaching a four-year high in April, Sygnum suggests that regulatory advancements in the United States, including initiatives by U.S. President Donald Trump to establish digital asset reserves and progress in stablecoin regulation, could foster broader cryptocurrency adoption.

Sygnum anticipates that protocols successfully engaging users will outperform the market, potentially leading to a decline in Bitcoin's dominance. The growing focus on economic value has intensified competition, spurring better product development that ultimately benefits consumers. The report highlights that while high-performance blockchains address limitations of Bitcoin, Ethereum, and Solana, they still face challenges in achieving widespread adoption and generating revenue.

data.title: BitMart Market Report on April 18: BTC is expected to continue to fluctuate in the short term
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News According to BitMart's market report on April 18, the total market value of the cryptocurrency market is 2.67 trillion US dollars, up 0.11% from the previous day. BTC fluctuated around $85,000 during the day, and BTCD surged to 63.2%; ETH followed the ups and downs of the market, fluctuating slightly below $1,600, and the ETH/BTC exchange rate fell below 0.019, continuing to hit a new low in the past five years; the historical lowest point of the ETH/BTC exchange rate was 0.0162 in September 2019. If the exchange rate falls below the historical low, ETH may face a greater sell-off; altcoins rose sporadically, and the market lacked collective narrative hotspots; the comprehensive long-short ratio was 1.06, and the multi-party forces were at a 24-hour high; investors need to pay attention to risks.

data.title: Goldman Sachs: Once the Fed becomes a political tool, it will push gold prices up to $4,500 an ounce
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News: In view of the fact that Trump has recently frequently sent danger signals to Federal Reserve Chairman Powell and threatened to fire him if he does not cut interest rates, analysts at Goldman Sachs warned that once the Fed loses its independence, the market will face extreme volatility.
Goldman Sachs expects that in extreme tail risk scenarios, such as increased market attention to the risks of Fed subordination or changes in US reserve policy, central bank demand continues to rise to 110 tons/month, the US recession brings ETF holdings to the level of the epidemic period, and speculative positions reach the top of the historical range, the gold price may be close to US$4,500/ounce by the end of 2025. (Wall Street News)

data.title: Trump Unlocks $300 Million Worth of Tokens, Impacting Circulation
data.description: According to BlockBeats, U.S. President Donald Trump unlocked 40 million tokens valued at approximately $300 million five hours ago. This amount represents 20% of the total circulation. Additionally, there is a daily linear unlocking of about 493,000 tokens, worth approximately $4.12 million.

data.title: VeChain Appoints UFC CEO Dana White and Nobel Laureate to Advisory Board
data.description: According to Foresight News, VeChain has announced the appointment of UFC CEO Dana White as an official advisor. Additionally, Nobel Prize-winning physicist Konstantin Novoselov has joined the advisory committee. Their combined expertise in public marketing and scientific innovation will focus on advancing real-world blockchain applications.
